title: Kintetsu Line derailment in Kyoto leaves service interrupted, no injuries
summary: A Kintetsu Line train derailed shortly after leaving Kyoto Station, with no injuries, but interrupting service between Kyoto and Kamitobaguchi.

title: Japan prepares for World Cup match against Brazil without Kubo
summary: Japan prepares to face Brazil in the World Cup, with coach Moriyasu seeking to make history and forward Kubo absent due to injury.

title: US and Iran agree to ceasefire and will negotiate in Qatar
summary: The United States and Iran have reportedly agreed to a cessation of hostilities and plan talks in Qatar on June 30, according to US media reports.

title: Two dead at adult venue in Kobe; knife found
summary: Two people died at an adult entertainment venue in Kobe, and a knife was found at the scene, suggesting a possible homicide.

title: Heatwave in Europe leaves more than 1,300 dead, according to WHO
summary: The heatwave in Europe has caused more than 1,300 deaths, with record temperatures in Germany and France, according to the WHO.

title: Russia attacks 740 religious sites in Ukraine and faces gasoline shortage
summary: Ukraine reported that Russia has attacked 740 religious sites, while the gasoline shortage in Russia and Russian military tactics are under analysis.

title: Life sentence sought for defendant in murder of 'Gyoza no Osho' president
summary: The prosecution sought life imprisonment for a gang member accused of murdering the president of the restaurant chain 'Gyoza no Osho' in Kyoto.

title: Man arrested for burying a man in Hiroshima in robbery and murder case
summary: A 29-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of robbery and murder after a buried body was found in the city of Mihara, Hiroshima.

title: South Korean president severely criticizes federation after World Cup elimination
summary: The president of South Korea strongly criticized the coach and the football federation after South Korea's elimination from the World Cup.

title: Canada beats South Africa in extra time and advances to World Cup round of 16
summary: Canada secured their place in the round of 16 by beating South Africa with a last-minute goal from Eustaquio.

title: Satoko Kishimoto re-elected as mayor of Tokyo's Suginami Ward
summary: Independent mayor Satoko Kishimoto was re-elected in Tokyo's Suginami Ward, defeating a candidate backed by the LDP.

title: New rankings for Nagoya sumo tournament: Kirishima promoted to ozeki
summary: The Sumo Association published the rankings for the Nagoya tournament, highlighting Kirishima's promotion to ozeki and Wakunakayama's return to sekiwake.

title: Baby rescued alive 32 hours after earthquake in Venezuela
summary: A baby was rescued 32 hours after the earthquake in Venezuela, which has left more than a thousand dead, according to reports.

title: Posthumous tributes to artist Miwa Akihiro in Japan
summary: Following the death of singer and actor Miwa Akihiro, his hometown of Nagasaki and entertainment colleagues pay tribute to him.

title: New images of suspect in Beppu hit-and-run
summary: Police released new surveillance images showing the vehicle of the suspect in the Beppu hit-and-run, who remains at large.

title: Japan and South Korea agree to strengthen defense cooperation
summary: The defense ministers of Japan and South Korea agreed to strengthen security cooperation and discussed the denuclearization of North Korea.

title: Hunter x Hunter to surpass 100 million copies in circulation
summary: The manga 'Hunter x Hunter' will reach 100 million copies sold with the publication of volume 39, which includes the first new chapter in a long time.

title: Nikkei falls more than 1,200 points in volatile session
summary: The Nikkei 225 index fell more than 1,200 points at the open, though it later partially recovered thanks to futures buying.

title: Survey reveals 43% do not accept Prime Minister Takaichi's explanation about defamatory video
summary: A survey shows that 43% are not satisfied with Prime Minister Takaichi's explanation about a defamatory video, while his support varies.

title: China bans export of dual-use items to Japan
summary: China announced a ban on exporting dual-use items to Japan, affecting 20 Japanese companies, amid bilateral tensions.
